title: FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE - 211info
text: URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights, eviction prevention, and legal assistance, referring cases requiring legal advice or representation by attorneys to Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also, conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 280-2600 Main phone Main phone (503) 280-2600 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for more information FEES: None for screening; fees for legal services at partner agencies vary DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah and Clackamas Counties Services: Eviction Prevention Assistance Legal Information Services * Tenants Specialized Information and Referral * Tenants Tenant Rights Information/Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADVOCACY AND CIVIC ENGAGEMENT COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE EMPLOYMENT - WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - COMMUNITY HEALTH HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON HEALTH PLAN COMMUNITY PARTNER SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - YOUTH PROGRAMS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND
